commit.message:
fix(ui): use event filters for undecorated window resize (#55)

* Initial plan

* fix(ui): use event filters for window resize to work at all edges

Switch from scene event handlers (setOnMouseMoved/Pressed/Dragged) to
event filters (addEventFilter) for undecorated window resize handling.

Event handlers fire during the bubbling phase, so child nodes like
TabPane and ScrollPane can consume drag events before they reach the
scene handler. Event filters fire during the capturing phase (before
child nodes), ensuring resize works at all window edges.

Also consume press/drag events when in resize mode to prevent child
node interference.

commit.message:
fix(dispatcher): canonicalize IPv4-mapped IPv6 UDP dst to prevent EINVAL on bind (#1312)

fix(dispatcher): canonicalize IPv4-mapped IPv6 UDP dst before socket creation

The SS2022 inbound path can produce packet.dst_addr as
SocksAddr::Ip(SocketAddr::V6(::ffff:x.x.x.x)) even when the original
ATYP was 0x01 (IPv4).  Without canonicalization, new_udp_socket sees
family_hint=Some([::ffff:1.1.1.1]:53) and creates an AF_INET6 socket,
but bind_addr is still 0.0.0.0 (AF_INET, derived from the IPv4 session
source).  Binding an IPv6 socket to 0.0.0.0:0 returns EINVAL (os
error 22), silently dropping all outbound UDP datagrams.

Fix: before stashing resolved_ip and before reverse_lookup, call
addr.to_canonical() (via the existing ToCanonical trait) to convert
::ffff:x.x.x.x to plain x.x.x.x.  This ensures both packet.dst_addr
and sess.resolved_ip carry a pure IPv4 address, so new_udp_socket
creates an AF_INET socket that can bind to 0.0.0.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add Ctrl+J as universal newline key for multiline input

Most macOS terminals (Terminal.app, iTerm2) send identical \r for both
Enter and Shift+Enter — there's no way to distinguish them. Only Kitty
and WezTerm support the Kitty keyboard protocol (\x1b[13;2u).

Fix: Add Ctrl+J (\n / LF) as cross-terminal newline. In raw mode, Enter
sends \r (CR) while Ctrl+J sends \n (LF), making them distinguishable
on ALL terminal emulators.

Newline options:
- Ctrl+J: Works everywhere (recommended)
- Shift+Enter: Works on Kitty/WezTerm (Kitty protocol)
- Alt+Enter: Works on iTerm2 and some terminals
- Backslash + Enter: Always works (manual continuation)

Also: added multiline hint to banner UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent 'Failed to fetch' on expired SWA auth sessions (#131)

* fix: prevent 'Failed to fetch' on expired SWA auth sessions (#130)

Root cause: When a user's SWA authentication cookie expires mid-session,
API calls to /api/converse get a 302 redirect to Azure AD's login page.
The browser's fetch() follows this cross-origin redirect, but Azure AD
doesn't return CORS headers, so the browser throws TypeError: Failed to
fetch — an opaque error with no actionable context for the user.

Fix:
- Add apiFetch() wrapper that uses redirect:'manual' to prevent the
  browser from silently following auth redirects across origins
- Detect opaque redirect responses and throw SessionExpiredError with
  a clear message instead of the cryptic 'Failed to fetch'
- Auto-redirect users to the SWA login page on session expiry
- Add error field to StreamEvent so SSE error events from the server
  are properly surfaced instead of silently swallowed
- Apply apiFetch to all authenticated API endpoints (converse,
  inspirations, playground)

Closes #130

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update Bender history + decision for SWA auth fix (#130)

- Cancel reader and abort controller on SSE error before returning

* docs: Merge chained pointer convention decision (Decision 21)

Added Decision 21 documenting the opt-in-only convention for
resolveChainedPointer: any string starting with '/' is followed,
while resolveDataPath never follows pointers. This prevents
accidental pointer following (e.g., file paths being treated
as data model pointers).

PR: #122

commit.message:
fix: handle async FSWatcher errors in project customization watcher

On Linux, fs.watch() on a non-existent directory emits an asynchronous
'error' event instead of throwing synchronously. Without an error handler,
this crashes the process. Tests that create AryxAppService with fake
project paths (e.g. C:\workspace\project-one) trigger this on Linux CI
because those paths don't exist.

Attach an 'error' event handler to the FSWatcher that logs a warning and
closes the watcher gracefully. This fixes the Linux CI 'bun test' failure
and also prevents production crashes if a watched directory is deleted
at runtime.
